summary: Iteration 47: P0 SCROLL BUG FIX VERIFICATION - ALL TESTS PASSED. The 3-layer JavaScript inline style fix is working correctly on https://cognition-overhaul.preview.emergentagent.com. Tested Desktop (1440x900), Mobile (390x844), and Visual scroll tests. All inline styles applied correctly (overflow-y: auto on html and body), scrollY values change correctly (0→800), and page has sufficient scrollable content.

layers_tested: Layer 0: IIFE in index.js (line 17-28) - Synchronous scroll unlock before React mounts
Layer 1: useEffect in App.js (line 145-166) - React-level scroll unlock with setTimeout re-apply
Layer 2: useEffect in LandingIntelligent.js (line 343-357) - Landing page specific scroll unlock

inline_styles_verified.body_getAttribute_style: overflow: hidden auto; height: auto; position: static;
inline_styles_verified.html_getAttribute_style: overflow: hidden auto; height: auto; position: static;

visual_scroll_test.screenshots_different: YES - Different content visible at top vs scrolled positions

action_items: SCROLL FIX VERIFIED - All tests pass on preview URL
If user still experiences scroll issues after deployment, advise:
1. Hard refresh with Ctrl+Shift+R (Windows) or Cmd+Shift+R (Mac)
4. Check if browser extensions are interfering
critical_code_review_comments: PASS: 3-layer JavaScript scroll fix correctly implemented
PASS: Layer 0 (index.js IIFE) runs synchronously before React mounts
PASS: Layer 1 (App.js useEffect) re-applies scroll unlock with 500ms timeout
PASS: Layer 2 (LandingIntelligent.js useEffect) applies scroll unlock on landing page mount
PASS: Inline styles override all CSS stylesheets (highest specificity)
PASS: Position set to 'static' to prevent scroll locking
PASS: Height set to 'auto' to allow natural content flow
INFO: Service worker kill code present to prevent caching issues
context_for_next_testing_agent: Scroll is VERIFIED WORKING on production preview URL with the 3-layer JavaScript inline style fix. All inline styles are correctly applied (overflow-y: auto on html and body). scrollY correctly changes from 0 to 800+ on both desktop and mobile. Page has plenty of scrollable content (4821px on desktop, 9242px on mobile). If user reports continued issues after this verification, it is likely browser cache, service worker, or external factors (browser extensions, network proxy).

rca_of_the_issue.original_report: P0 CRITICAL - Landing page scroll completely broken, users cannot scroll at all
rca_of_the_issue.root_cause: CSS rules or JavaScript code was previously blocking overflow-y on html/body elements
rca_of_the_issue.fix_implemented: 3-layer JavaScript inline style fix that sets document.body.style.overflowY = 'auto' and document.documentElement.style.overflowY = 'auto' - inline styles cannot be overridden by any CSS stylesheet
rca_of_the_issue.verification_result: FIX CONFIRMED WORKING - All scroll tests pass with correct scrollY values and inline styles applied
rca_of_the_issue.conclusion: The scroll functionality is working correctly on the preview URL. The 3-layer fix ensures scroll is enabled: (1) before React mounts, (2) when App component mounts, (3) when landing page mounts.
